title: taenitis rigida
intro: Taenitis rigida, also known as the iron grass, is a perennial fern native to New Zealand and other Pacific islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and has been used to treat conditions like arthritis and wounds. In traditional medicine, Taenitis rigida is often prepared as a tea or infused oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
